title: dendrobium fulgidum subsp. maritimum
intro: Dendrobium fulgidum subsp. maritimum, also known as the coastal dendrobium, is a type of orchid that is native to tropical regions, particularly in the coastal areas of Australia and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of the coastal dendrobium include treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic to prevent infection. Herbal preparations of the coastal dendrobium can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
